title: Colony peer-to-peer interaction dormant — DOMEX bundles superseded but knowledge boundary persists
hypothesis: Colony active signal rate unchanged since S307 (~10.8%). DOMEX lanes provide the actual inter-domain interaction.

measurements.window: S307-S417 (110 sessions since last F-EXP6 update)

expect_vs_actual.expected_domex_supersession: DOMEX lanes handle most inter-domain work
expect_vs_actual.unexpected_finding: 0.1% cross-domain lesson citations — sessions interact through shared context window but produce domain-siloed knowledge

key_finding.summary: Three-layer interaction architecture: (1) Colony SIGNALS.md: dormant, 11.6%, 0 growth. (2) DOMEX session bundling: active, 75% multi-domain. (3) Knowledge graph: zero cross-domain citations (0.1%). Layer 2 superseded layer 1, but layer 3 reveals the domain boundary persists in knowledge production.
key_finding.mechanism: Multi-domain DOMEX sessions share a context window (implicit interaction), but each lesson is filed under a single domain and cites within-domain lessons only. The domain tag on a lesson acts as a citation attractor — authors cite what they can see in the domain frontier, not across domains.
key_finding.implication: Colony infrastructure (41 COLONY.md, 5 SIGNALS.md) is dead capital. DOMEX bundles solved the dispatch problem (getting sessions to work on multiple domains) but not the knowledge integration problem (getting lessons to cross-cite). L-926 namespace disconnection at the frontier level has a lesson-level analogue.
key_finding.connection_to_L926: L-926 found 95.9% of domain frontiers have no parent global link. This experiment shows 99.9% of lessons have no cross-domain citation. Both are symptoms of the same domain-boundary citation barrier.

recommendations.colony_triage: ABANDON colony SIGNALS.md as interaction mechanism. 110 sessions of zero growth = dead protocol.
recommendations.cross_domain_citation_mechanism: When lesson_quality_fixer.py --fix adds Cites: headers, also suggest cross-domain citations from same-session lessons in different domains.
recommendations.frontier_resolution: F-EXP6 can be PARTIALLY RESOLVED: colony interaction mode is dead, replaced by DOMEX bundling + implicit context sharing. Open question: can explicit cross-domain citation mechanism close the 0.1% gap?
